english: Fresh Coriander Salad with Sesame Dressing

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for coriander salad; 'salad food product' (FOODON_00002539) used as the closest parent class. Coriander as ingredient is represented by FOODON_00003044 (coriander).

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific composite code for Japanese-style coriander salad. A00LC (coriander leaves) used as the closest parent ingredient anchor. Composite dish classification not available in FoodEx2.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard side dish serving of coriander salad is approximately 150 g, typically served as a starter or accompaniment in a Japanese-style meal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Coriander (cilantro) leaves, raw (FDC ID 169997) and Salad dressing, sesame seed (FDC ID 172459); composite dish estimated from ingredient proportions.
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No approved source contains a direct entry for Japanese-style coriander salad with sesame and chili dressing. Nutrient values were estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for raw coriander leaves (FDC ID 169997) and sesame-based dressing components, cross-referenced against the input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7). Input values were retained where consistent with USDA component data. Energy 120 kcal/100g, protein 3.0g, fat 9.33g, carbohydrate 6.0g are plausible for a coriander salad with sesame oil dressing at approximately 60–65% coriander and 35–40% dressing by weight. Sodium 300 mg/100g reflects soy sauce and chili sauce contribution. All values treated as estimated.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on mixed green salad with dressing (GI ~32).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Japanese-style coriander salad. GI estimated at 32 (low) based on the closest analogous food: mixed green salad with oil-based dressing from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Coriander is a non-starchy leafy vegetable with negligible starch; sesame oil and vinegar dressing components have no meaningful GI contribution. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (32 × 6.0 / 100) × 1.5 = 2.88, rounded to 2.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg per 100g from soy sauce and chili sauce components
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100g or less and request reduced-sodium soy sauce, as the 300 mg/100g sodium content contributes approximately 450 mg per standard 150g serving, representing 20% of the 2,000 mg daily sodium target recommended by the Singapore HPB.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (6.0g/100g), low sugar (2.67g/100g), and low estimated GI (~32)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 32 and only 6.0g carbohydrate per 100g, this salad produces a glycemic load of approximately 2.9 per 150g serving, making it a suitable side dish for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; however, chili sauce should be checked for added sugars and used sparingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.33g/100g), zero trans fat, and anti-inflammatory sesame and coriander components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content (1.33g/100g), absence of trans fat, and presence of sesame lignans and coriander polyphenols support a heart-healthy profile; sodium at 300 mg/100g should be monitored in individuals with concurrent hypertension or heart failure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory coriander and sesame components; chili sauce contains nightshade-family ingredients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Coriander provides vitamin C (13.33mg/100g) and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties; individuals with RA who are sensitive to nightshades should substitute chili sauce with rice vinegar to avoid potential solanine-related joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coriander Salad (パクチーサラダ, Pakuchī sarada) is a Japanese-style fresh salad featuring raw coriander (cilantro) leaves as the primary ingredient, tossed with a dressing of sesame oil, sesame seeds, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and chili sauce. The dish is served cold as a side dish or starter, offering a bright, herbaceous flavour with a nutty sesame undertone and a mild chili heat. The texture is crisp and fresh, characteristic of minimally processed raw leafy salads. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides approximately 120 kcal, 3.0g protein, 9.33g fat (predominantly unsaturated from sesame oil), 6.0g carbohydrate, 2.67g sugar, 2.0g dietary fibre, and 300mg sodium. Calcium is 53.33mg/100g and vitamin C is 13.33mg/100g, reflecting the nutrient density of fresh coriander. The dish is vegan, vegetarian, and dairy-free, but contains sesame and soy allergens. Its low glycemic index (estimated ~32) and low glycemic load (~2.9 per 150g serving) make it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content warrants caution for hypertension patients. The dish is commonly served in Japanese restaurants in Singapore as a side accompaniment to ramen or other noodle dishes.
common_user_queries: How many calories in coriander salad?
Is coriander salad good for diabetics?
What allergens are in coriander salad?

confidence_reason: No approved primary source contains a direct entry for Japanese-style coriander salad with sesame and chili dressing. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for raw coriander leaves and sesame dressing ingredients, cross-referenced against the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7). GI is an analogous estimate. All values carry inherent uncertainty due to variable dressing ratios and preparation methods.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16, as no direct entry exists for Japanese-style coriander salad. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish preparation is not directly available in an approved source; USDA reports ~521mg/100g for raw coriander leaves but the composite dish value cannot be reliably derived without confirmed dressing ratios. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in any approved source consulted. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data for this composite dish is not available in approved sources as a prepared dish entry. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — sesame oil and plant-based dressing components contain no trans fats per USDA FoodData Central. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — all ingredients are plant-based with no animal-derived cholesterol sources. The input record's ethnic_tags ('Ramen', 'Noodle') appear inconsistent with a coriander salad dish and have been reclassified; the dish is retained as Japanese cuisine based on the primary_cuisine field. Input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) does not correspond to the HPB Foodo nutrient database and has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central as the approved nutrient source.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from input; added multilingual names, USDA nutrient source, GI estimate, health context, and ontology anchors.
Iteration 2: Added mandatory Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to resolve G6 failure; corrected ontology match types.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' to use only valid enum values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'], resolving the G5 hard gate failure reported by the validator.
